mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving immediate positive momentum in equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and nonfarm payrolls jointly signal aggregate demand strength; when both series rise simultaneously, corporate revenue growth accelerates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and PAYEMS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth directly expands corporate capacity utilization and profit margins; during periods of sustained supply expansion, S&P 500 forward returns turn positive as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth correlates negatively with SPX 20-day returns during confirmed capacity expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential construction financing signals deteriorating credit conditions and housing demand, triggering immediate risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines more than 5 percent over two months
failure_condition: RSAFS declines do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and unemployment rate indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown; this dual pressure compresses equity valuations and earnings expectations, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Combined FEDFUNDS and UNRATE increases fail to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth serves as a fundamental earnings anchor; positive quarterly GDP expansion consistently drives upward revisions to corporate profit forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no monotonic relationship with 20-day SPX returns across multiple decades of data.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly employment gains fall below 100,000 persons for three consecutive months, labor demand deterioration signals an impending earnings recession, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS adds fewer than 100,000 persons for three consecutive months
failure_condition: PAYEMS prints below 100k for three months do not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of instances.
